what_it_does: Acceptance test module that exercises the guide:// address scheme, GuideRegistry discovery/rediscovery, resolve_address dispatch, mandatory grounding/target validation, drift reflection in guides/AGENTS.md, and the authoring render/gate path for guide entries.
observations: Defines a global PASS counter and two helpers, check() and raises(), for assert-style acceptance checks.
Imports contracts.address SCHEMES/scheme, FsStore, resolve_address, GuideRegistry, GuideEntry, _build_guide, and runtime.authoring.
Verifies that the guide scheme is added to SCHEMES without removing skill/context/run/cas.
Instantiates GuideRegistry, discovers entries from the guides/ directory, and asserts the seed guide is found by id.
Dynamically writes and removes a probe guide file in guides/ to prove discovery and rediscovery behavior.
Skips _-prefixed files by writing _skipme.py and asserting it is not registered.
Uses a temporary FsStore to test resolve_address('guide://using_skills') and fail-loud resolution of an unknown guide.
Calls _build_guide directly with malformed rows to enforce fail-loud grounding, target, id, content, and schema rules.
Reads guides/AGENTS.md and checks that every discovered guide id appears in it.
Tests authoring render_entry_source and gate_entry_source for guides, including author-time grounding gates.
standouts: Dynamic proof by writing/removing real files in guides/ to validate registry behavior without mocks or live models.
Mandatory grounding gate tested at both build time and author-time render.
Drift check that ties every discovered guide back to guides/AGENTS.md.
Explicitly mirrors SkillRegistry patterns (discover/rediscover/register/read) rather than inventing a parallel shape.
conventions: Follows a long explanatory docstring that names the laws the suite is intended to prove.
Uses sys.path insertion and os.chdir(ROOT) at module load time.
Uses module-level constants ROOT, GUIDES, SEED_GUIDE, and PASS.
Uses a lightweight custom check helper instead of a framework like pytest.
Performs a late, section-local import of runtime.authoring inside section 6.
Cleans up temporary probe files in a finally block.
concerns: Writes temporary probe files directly into the real guides/ directory; incomplete cleanup could leave artifacts.
os.chdir(ROOT) mutates the global working directory for the process.
check() relies on a global PASS counter and is not thread-safe.
Late import of authoring inside a function makes dependencies less visible.
The test only reads AGENTS.md; it does not verify that the guide content is accurately described, only that ids appear.
notes: An acceptance test that treats guides as first-class, discoverable, fail-loud registry entries and verifies their scheme, discovery, resolution, grounding, drift, and authoring gates.
questions: What is the exact field set and validation logic inside GuideEntry and _build_guide?
How does GuideRegistry._load_module compare to SkillRegistry._load_module?
What is the precise format expected in guides/AGENTS.md beyond containing each guide id?
How does resolve_address dispatch guide:// paths to GuideRegistry.read?
What is the full list of SCHEMES and the behavior of scheme() for edge cases?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the actual content: it executes the described acceptance checks by mechanism.
apparent_themes: Drift reflection between code and documentation
intention_signals: To establish guides as first-class, discoverable resources with the same structural guarantees as skills, enforce grounding at build and authoring time, and keep the narrative drift home synchronized with the registry.
novelty: It dynamically writes and deletes real files in the production guides/ directory as a proof of discovery/rediscovery, and combines runtime resolution tests with authoring-time gating in one acceptance suite.
contribution: An executable contract test that documents and verifies guide:// scheme registration, GuideRegistry semantics, mandatory grounding/target validation, drift-home linkage, and the guide authoring path.
summary_for_embedding: company/tests/guides_acceptance.py is an acceptance test suite that validates the guide:// address scheme, GuideRegistry file discovery and rediscovery, resolve_address dispatch, mandatory grounding and target validation, drift reflection in guides/AGENTS.md, and authoring render/gate behavior.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to prove by mechanism that narrative guides are addressable, discoverable, fail-loud registry entries grounded in real sources and reflected in project documentation.

symbols.does: Asserts a boolean condition, increments PASS, and prints an ok message for each acceptance check.
symbols.description: A lightweight test helper used throughout the suite; it takes a label and a condition, asserts the condition with the label as the failure message, increments the global PASS counter on success, and prints a confirmation line.

symbols.does: Verifies that a callable raises an exception, used to assert fail-loud behavior.
symbols.description: A helper that invokes the provided callable and passes only if any exception is raised; used to validate that malformed or ungrounded guide entries fail loudly at build and authoring time.
